raw:
{
  "judge_id": "Judge-1",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.4", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 3,
    "R1.2": 5,
    "R1.4": 3,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 1
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The abstract does not strictly restate the surface phrasing without changing the conceptual object. It identifies one conceptual issue (direct self-contradiction) and proposes a reframing, but it is partially supported.",
    "R1.2": "The output walks the chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency, and proposes minimal repairs.",
    "R1.4": "The output names one prior work (Locatello et al.) and articulates the delta, but it does not map against the nearest 3-5 works; identifies sharp vs rhetorical delta.",
    "RX.1": "The output flags uncertainty for one or two clearly out-of-reach claims (direct self-contradiction and Locatello et al.'s impossibility result), but it does not mark confidence and missing evidence for each recommendation.",
    "RX.3": "Output covers exactly the agreed J*(d).",
    "RX.4": "No regressions; identifies harmful patterns in the original (direct self-contradiction and Locatello et al.'s impossibility result).",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is not annotated with source and reason."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.8,
    "R1.2": 0.9,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 0.8,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 0.6
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The abstract does not strictly restate the surface phrasing without changing the conceptual object. It identifies one conceptual issue (direct self-contradiction) and proposes a reframing, but it is partially supported.
rationale.R1.2: The output walks the chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency, and proposes minimal repairs.
rationale.R1.4: The output names one prior work (Locatello et al.) and articulates the delta, but it does not map against the nearest 3-5 works; identifies sharp vs rhetorical delta.
rationale.RX.1: The output flags uncertainty for one or two clearly out-of-reach claims (direct self-contradiction and Locatello et al.'s impossibility result), but it does not mark confidence and missing evidence for each recommendation.
rationale.RX.3: Output covers exactly the agreed J*(d).
rationale.RX.4: No regressions; identifies harmful patterns in the original (direct self-contradiction and Locatello et al.'s impossibility result).
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is not annotated with source and reason.
